spec_match.rationale: The page now avoids the stale position-try-options syntax and uses current anchor positioning with position-try-order: most-height.

interactive.rationale: The toggle compares CSS anchor positioning with a legacy JS fallback and the scroller remains directly manipulable.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page demonstrates remembered scroll offset instead of generic anchor positioning alone.

edge_cases.rationale: The page directly probes anchor support and fallback-order support while showing the legacy fallback path.

references.rationale: The page links to chromestatus.com/feature/4710507824807936 and the CSS Anchor Positioning spec.

design_system.rationale: The local styling uses shared CSS variables and accessible status colors.

summary: The single-scroller demo now reflects current syntax and browser support, replacing the stale most-scroll-position framing.
